text.title: Feature Prioritization RICE Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Feature Prioritization RICE application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Reach MUST cite a named unit (users/mo OR sessions/Q OR accounts/Y); unit-less Reach is rejected.

rationale.source: Sean McBride — RICE Scoring at Intercom

rule.rationale: Reach drift across features makes ranking apples-to-oranges.

rule.statement: Impact MUST be 1 / 2 / 3 (or 0.25 / 0.5 / 1) with cited anchor example per level; free-floating Impact is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without anchor, Impact becomes vibe; anchors keep teams calibrated.

rule.statement: Confidence MUST be high / medium / low with cited source (analytics URL / interview ids / experiment id); uncited Confidence is rejected.
rule.rationale: Default-100% Confidence inflates every score; citation makes Confidence falsifiable.

rule.statement: Effort MUST be in person-weeks (or hours, locked per scoring round); mixed units rejected.
rule.rationale: Mixed units make RICE rank meaningless.

rule.statement: Output MUST declare a cut-line (e.g. RICE > X stays, < X parked); unspecified cut line is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without cut line the ranked list is observable but unactionable.
